title: Cold Shock Therapy
summary:
We had planned to head to the McKenzie River area next, so we bumped that up a day and decided to try our luck at Paradise Campground for the night. We ended up finding a delightful spot right on the river. The McKenzie River is a cold, stunning, deep blue river that meanders along the road for about 30 miles. We spent the next few days exploring different sections of the river.

Our first hike was up to something called the Blue Pool. It was one of those rare instances when the water in the pool actually looked bluer in person than in the pictures. It looked so inviting that we decided to take a dip. We quickly learned that the reason for the deep blue color is that it is so cold, no microorganisms or anything can live in it (about 38 degrees, brrr!). After recovering and drying off in the sun, we had a nice walk out, and we were luckily on our way to some hot springs. We spent the night at Belknap Hot Springs, which has RV sites that give you access to their hot springs pools. The pools were not glamorous but I was happy to spend some time reading and warming up in the hot water. We made sure to take advantage of the hot springs and did another soak in the morning before heading out!

The following day, we mapped out a longer hike/run along the river trail to see a couple of waterfalls (Sahalie and Koosah falls) and run around Clear Lake. The waterfalls were epic, the trail was nice, and I did my longest run ever (9 miles, very slow, with a good amount of walking)! That night, we met back up with Abby at Blue Bay campground, where we played some Wingspan and ate some yummy pasta and tiramisu that Abby had bought.

-Helen

Helen taking a cold plunge in the blue pool.
Maya snoozing while we watched the movie Bugonia in bed.
